name: Key Hint
description: Inline keyboard shortcut indicator shown in the header bar and panel action slots. Renders a key name inside square brackets in a dim monospace style. Optional hints fade further (var(--color-text-muted)) when not universally relevant. Conveys the keyboard-driven ethos of the TUI without clutter.

anatomy.description: Square brackets [ ] wrapping the key name. Color var(--color-text-muted)
The key itself, var(--text-xs), color var(--color-text-secondary) for primary / var(--color-text-muted) for optional
Optional trailing action word in the user's language, same color as key-name, no brackets

structurePatterns: display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1)
Bracket + key rendered as literal text '[Esc]', not separate DOM elements (simpler, preserves TUI feel)
usageHints: Cluster hints right-aligned in the header bar, separated by var(--space-3)
Optional hints can be hidden behind a toggle on narrow viewports
Plus-chain format (Ctrl+P) uses literal + inside the brackets
doNotInvent: Do not add keycap-shaped backgrounds or borders — this is not a GUI keycap component
Do not color the key with the accent color; it must stay muted to avoid visual noise
